sections.paragraph:
Γάδ, ὁ indecl. (גָּד) Gad (Gen 30:11; 49:19; Test12Patr, JosAs; Philo, Somn. 2, 34.—In Joseph. Γάδας [Ant. 1, 306] or Γάδης [Ant. 2, 182], ου [Ant. 6, 99]) φυλὴ Γ. Rv 7:5 (s. Δάν).

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 186
